name: google-forms
description: Read Google Forms structure and responses, and create or edit forms

tools.name: Get Form
tools.description: Get a form's metadata, settings, and items (questions) including the question IDs needed to interpret responses

formId.description: Form ID (the long ID in the form's edit URL)

tools.name: List Form Responses
tools.description: List submitted responses for a form, with answers keyed by question ID

filter.description: Response filter, e.g. timestamp >= 2024-01-01T00:00:00Z (only the timestamp field is supported)

pageToken.description: Page token from a previous list call

tools.name: Get Form Response
tools.description: Get a single response submitted to a form

tools.name: Create Form
tools.description: Create a new form with a title; add questions afterwards with Batch Update Form

info.description: Form info object, e.g. { title: 'Customer feedback', documentTitle: 'Feedback form' }. Only title and documentTitle are honored at creation.

tools.name: Set Publish Settings
tools.description: Publish or unpublish a form and toggle whether it accepts responses, e.g. to open a survey after building it or close it when done (requires the forms.body scope)

publishSettings.description: Desired publish settings; publishState requires both flags, e.g. { "publishState": { "isPublished": true, "isAcceptingResponses": true } }. Setting isPublished false forces isAcceptingResponses to false.

updateMask.description: Optional field mask of publish_settings fields to update, e.g. publishState (defaults to all fields)

tools.name: Batch Update Form
tools.description: Apply a batch of updates to a form, such as adding questions, updating form info, or changing settings

requests.description: Array of update request objects, e.g. [{ createItem: { item: {...}, location: { index: 0 } } }] or [{ updateFormInfo: { info: {...}, updateMask: 'description' } }]

includeFormInResponse.description: Return the updated form in the response

writeControl.description: Optional write control, e.g. { requiredRevisionId: '...' } to guard against concurrent edits

prompts.title: Summarize form responses
prompts.prompt: Get the responses for my Google Form and summarize the answers per question, highlighting common themes.

prompts.title: Create a feedback form
prompts.prompt: Create a new Google Form for customer feedback and add a rating question plus an open-text comments question.

prompts.title: Recent submissions
prompts.prompt: Show me the responses submitted to my Google Form in the last 7 days.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Google Forms setup

steps.title: Create a Google Cloud project
steps.description: Go to https://console.cloud.google.com, create (or select) a project. A free Google account is enough for testing.

steps.title: Enable the Google Forms API
steps.description: Open https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/library/forms.googleapis.com and click Enable for your project.

steps.title: Configure the OAuth consent screen
steps.description: Under APIs & Services > OAuth consent screen, configure the app and add the Forms scopes (forms.body.readonly, forms.responses.readonly, and forms.body if you need write access). Add your account as a test user while the app is in testing mode.

steps.title: Create OAuth credentials
steps.description: Under APIs & Services > Credentials, create an OAuth client ID of type Web application and add your callback URL as an authorized redirect URI. Set GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID and GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET from the generated credentials.

notes: The Forms API can read responses but cannot submit them; responses are only created by people filling out the form.
Create Form only honors info.title and info.documentTitle; add questions and settings afterwards with Batch Update Form.
Creating or editing forms requires the https://www.googleapis.com/auth/forms.body scope (listed under optional scopes).
